rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Assumptions and guarantees for component ports belong in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annexes only in valid scopes matching component type or implementation.
Use only visible identifiers from the current AADL component scope in AGREE contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Avoid unsafe Boolean or numeric encodings of event ports or opaque data types in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings must appear in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level AGREE bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
